attacking_style: Does not feature a primary attacking rider profile; instead focuses on positioning sprinters and puncheurs for reduced bunch finishes after selective terrain.
team_tactics: Employs full domestique deployment on selective climbs to create puncheur-sprint scenarios; controls breakaway margins tightly (under 1:30) rather than building traditional leadouts; uses aggressive physical positioning in final kilometers.
terrain_preference: Stages with late climbs that eliminate pure sprinters but allow puncheur-sprinters to contest reduced finishes; moderately selective parcours over flat sprints or mountain stages.
vulnerability: Overmatched by superior sprint trains (Quick-Step) in pure leadout battles; forced into UCI points hunting via breakaways when out of GC contention, indicating resource constraints.
key_patterns: Breakaway margin control under 1:30 to engineer reduced sprint finishes (2 observations)
Full squad commitment on final climbs to position puncheur-sprinters, not pure leadouts (2 observations)
Aggressive physical positioning/blocking tactics inside final 1500m (2 observations)
UCI points urgency driving breakaway placement when GC irrelevant (2 observations across Grand Tours)
Targets stages with late selective terrain eliminating pure sprinters (3 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Consistent tactical approach across 2021-2022 focused on puncheur-sprint setups and points hunting, but no evolution in capabilities against top sprint trains
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 17
